scenarios.name: Credit: HIGH risk, same confidence
scenarios.description: Risk tier flips to HIGH but confidence stays at 0.82 (above the 0.60 APPROVE floor). DecisionGenerated carries causation_id pointing to CreditAnalysisCompleted, so it is suppressed in the counterfactual. ApplicationApproved is transitively suppressed. Divergences: risk_tier (MEDIUM vs HIGH), decision (APPROVE vs None), state (APPROVED vs SUBMITTED).

scenarios.name: Credit: HIGH risk + confidence below 0.60 floor
scenarios.description: Risk tier is HIGH and confidence drops to 0.55, below the 0.60 hard floor that forces REFER in handle_generate_decision. DecisionGenerated (causation_id = CreditAnalysisCompleted event_id) is causally suppressed in the counterfactual. The projector correctly shows decision=None rather than fabricating a REFER outcome - the correct cascade signal is suppression, not agent re-execution. Divergences: risk_tier, decision, state.

scenarios.name: Fraud: score above 0.60 threshold, recommendation flips to REFER
scenarios.description: Fraud score jumps from 0.05 to 0.75, crossing the 0.60 hard policy threshold. DecisionGenerated.causation_id points to CreditAnalysisCompleted (not FraudScreeningCompleted), so it is NOT suppressed when branching at fraud - it passes through as an independent event. Divergence is in the read model only: fraud_score (0.05 vs 0.75). This demonstrates single-parent causation: one event can carry one causation_id.
